notes: Per-treatment evidence commentary that the CMF library ingester merges into each entry by id. Kept separate from pipeline/build_cmf_library.py so domain experts can edit the prose without touching Python source.

by_id.cycle_track_at_intersection: Clearinghouse evidence: 4 studies measuring 'install cycle tracks, bike lanes, or on-street cycling' at urban intersections.
by_id.offset_cycle_track_with_priority: European urban evidence (single study). Offset cycle track design that maintains cyclist priority at junctions, in contrast to standard pulled-back designs.
by_id.raised_bike_crossing: Single-study European evidence. Effect operates via vehicle speed reduction at the conflict point.
by_id.install_bike_lane_generic: WARNING: studies show extreme heterogeneity (AMF range 0.16 to 1.43 across 4 studies). The averaged estimate masks substantial context-dependence — context where painted lanes help vs. hurt is not yet well characterised. Treat as low-confidence even though the mean is helpful.
by_id.prohibit_right_turn_on_red: Clearinghouse cmid 114 measures the *permit* direction: permitting RTOR increases bike crashes ~77% across 8 studies. The recommended action is the inverse (prohibition), implied by inverting the AMF.
by_id.convert_yield_to_signalized: 10 urban studies. Mean effect on bike crashes is essentially zero (AMF ~1.04). Strong vehicle-KSI evidence does not transfer to bike-KSI here. List in the library so SDOT can see the bike-evidence honestly.
by_id.bike_lane_at_signalized_intersection: 8 studies. Mean AMF ~1.08 — installing bike lanes at signalized intersections, on average, does not reduce observed bike crashes. Likely interpretation: facility raises bike exposure proportionally to risk reduction. This is why bike-volume (Strava Metro) is the critical missing covariate.
by_id.convert_to_roundabout_single_lane: ANTI-INDICATION for bike-KSI. 25 studies, mean AMF ~1.59 — single-lane roundabouts INCREASE bike-intersection crashes ~59% relative to the conventional intersection they replace. This is the well-documented bike-roundabout paradox: roundabouts are safer for cars and pedestrians but more dangerous for cyclists due to entry/exit conflict geometry. The 'cmf' field below is the studied AMF as-measured; do NOT mis-read this as a recommendation. Surfaced in the library so SDOT planners considering a roundabout retrofit see the bike-specific evidence.
by_id.lpi_signal_timing: Leading Pedestrian Interval (LPI). Gives pedestrians a 3-7 second head start when entering the crosswalk. Proven to reduce pedestrian-vehicle crashes by increasing visibility and establishing pedestrian right-of-way.
by_id.road_diet: Convert 4-lane undivided to 3-lane (2 through lanes + center turn lane). Reduces vehicle conflict points, lowers speeds, and provides space for pedestrian refuge islands or bike lanes.
